Empires & Puzzles Yona Review: The Gambling Bear of Clash of Knights

Meet Yona, the Clash of Knights bear who decides your fate with a coin flip. Will she save your team with massive overheals or cripple the enemy with permanent damage? We dive into the stats, the RNG, and why this fast-mana gambler might just be the chaotic energy your 3-star roster needs.
Yona Review: When Winnie the Pooh Picks Up a Gambling Habit
Yona: The Jester of the Highlands who Plays Dice with Your Life
Yona is a Rare (3-star) Nature hero from the

. Released back in early 2024, she brings a unique "Gambler" mechanic to the 3-star arena. Unlike the reliable healers of the past, Yona asks you a simple question: "Do you feel lucky?" She is a Fast mana support hero who flips a coin every time she fires—either healing your team or crippling the enemy. While most players prefer consistency, Yona's potential upside at Fast speed makes her a chaotic but powerful option, especially when you need a miracle to turn the tide.

Yona sports a jester-themed outfit complete with a bear-head staff and a hand of cards, clearly leaning into the "trickster" archetype. The green and blue color palette fits her Nature element, but it's the mischievous grin that really sells the character. She looks like she’s about to pull a rabbit out of a hat, or perhaps just a very angry badger. It's a refreshing change from the usual "stoic warrior staring into the middle distance" pose we see on so many cards.

This is the heart of the Yona experience: a 50/50 split that you cannot control.
This is essentially a "Mini-Heimdall" effect. A

heal at Fast speed is incredible for a 3-star hero. It can overheal your team early in the match, providing a massive safety buffer. The ramping Defense buff (+20% to +40%) makes your team incredibly sturdy.
This is the "Mini-Fura" effect. Reducing enemy Max Health by
is permanent damage (mostly). Against teams with healers, this is devastating because they can't heal it back. The ramping Defense down (-20% to -40%) softens them up for your snipers.

You can't choose. If your team is dying and needs a heal, Yona might decide to debuff the enemy instead. If you are trying to finish off a low-HP enemy, she might decide to heal your full-HP team. This unreliability effectively disqualifies her from "safe" offense teams, but makes her a nightmare on defense where chaos favors the AI.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Bear Family bonus is one of the strongest defensive tools in the game. Any attempt to lower Yona's defense is not just blocked; it's
into a Defense Buff. This makes her incredibly resilient against common defense-down heroes like
, turning their greatest strength into her advantage.

every time she receives a buff adds up quickly. In "Buff Booster" tournaments, she becomes a self-sustaining engine. If paired with other buffers (like Grevle or Faiez), she passively regenerates health every few turns.

Upon release, Yona was seen as a fun but risky alternative to steady healers like Belith or Grevle. She never quite dominated the meta like Treevil, but she found a home in specific niche defenses.
The "Fast" speed saves her. While her stats are lower than the 2025/2026 generation, the utility of
does not rely on her Attack stat. A 300 HP heal is still a 300 HP heal.
tournaments (thanks to her passive and family bonus). In standard play, the RNG is too risky for competitive offense, but she remains a solid "wildcard" tank for 3-star tournaments.

Fast mana allows her to impact the board early and often.
One of the few 3-star sources of Boosted Health, effectively countering max-HP reduction teams.
Max HP reduction is a powerful tool against stalling healer teams.
Immunity (and reversal) of Defense Down ailments is a top-tier defensive perk.
The 50/50 split is her fatal flaw. You cannot plan your turn around her special skill.
You cannot run her as your primary healer on offense because she might choose to attack when you are at 10 HP.
She is significantly squishier than the newest wave of 3-star monsters.
At Very Fast speed, she becomes a machine gun of random effects. The sheer volume of casts averages out the RNG.
Her passive heal and the defensive buffs she provides make her very sticky.
The Max HP reduction can be useful for bosses with massive health pools if you lack other options.
Yona works best when you embrace the chaos or mitigate the risk.

Faiez provides the reliability (Block Ailments), Grevle provides the overheal cap, and Yona acts as the fast-cycle engine. If she heals, great (more overheal). If she attacks, she softens the enemy for tile damage.
Pairing her with another Bear family member (like Torben if you have him) activates the family bonus for both, making your front line immune to defense down.
slows her down enough that she might die before firing.
Do not pair her with "Berserk" heroes or heroes that rely on low health, as her sudden heals can ruin their thresholds. Also, don't rely on her as a solo healer.
If you pull Yona early, level her! A fast healer (even a 50% one) is invaluable in the early game. The overheal mechanic allows your 3-star team to punch above its weight class in map stages.

She is a "for fun" hero. If you are competing for Top 100 in Challenge Events, she is too unpredictable. However, for 3-star Raid Tournaments, she is a fantastic "trap" hero to put on defense. The enemy attacker can't predict her either.
The community has always had a love-hate relationship with Yona.
"Draw a card for a specific effect... that essentially means the hero is bad. You can't rely on it."
- A common sentiment among competitive players who value consistency above all else.
"If she was Average speed, I wouldn't look at her. But Fast speed? Got to have a little fun, man."
- Proof that some players just want to watch the board burn (or heal).
"Interesting 3-star tank... could replace Faiez in no-red tournaments."
- Highlighting her niche as a Faiez alternative when the slow monk is too slow or banned.

Yona is the slot machine of Empires & Puzzles. She is not the hero you bring when you need a surgical strike; she is the hero you bring when you want to throw a wrench in the gears. While her stats are getting dusty in 2026, her ability to overheal or permanent-damage at Fast speed keeps her relevant in the 3-star tournament meta. If you can tolerate the gamble, she pays out often enough to be worth a roster spot.
Level her for tournaments, but don't bet your war hits on her.
